channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Understanding Remission of Long-Term Conditions Through Electronic Health Records: Scoping Review.
item.description: Journal of medical Internet research - Review - Multiple long-term conditions (MLTCs) require complex and prolonged treatment regimens. Remission in long-term conditions (LTCs) is important for understanding disease progression and evaluating tr...

item.title: Selenium-GPX4 Axis Orchestrates Intestinal Arachidonic Acid Metabolic Reprogramming to Mitigate Inflammation.
item.description: Free radical biology & medicine - Dietary westernization exacerbates polyunsaturated fatty acid-mediated intestinal inflammation, though the mechanistic links between arachidonic acid (AA)-induced gut metabolic dysregulation and gl...

item.title: Effect of Acceptance and Commitment Therapy on Psychological Symptoms and Disease Activity in Patients with Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Clinics and research in hepatology and gastroenterology - Review - Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) negatively impacts quality of life, increasing the risk of psychological disorders. Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) is a form of psychotherapy that has show...

item.title: Impact of different stress paradigms on irritable bowel syndrome: Evidence from clinical and animal studies.
item.description: The Journal of international medical research - Review - Irritable bowel syndrome is a chronic functional gastrointestinal disorder characterized by recurrent abdominal pain and altered bowel habits, a condition significantly influenced by stress. Based ...

item.title: Mapping the evolution of inflammatory bowel disease care in Brazil: a comparison of cross-sectional surveys from 2017 and 2023.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - In Brazil, the epidemiology and care pathways of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are poorly documented. Mapping the patient journey is key to identifying gaps and informing health po...

item.title: In vivo and molecular docking studies of Micromeria imbricata var. imbricata extract reveal alleviation of the DSS-induced IBD phenotype via interaction with key inflammatory mediators.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Satureja punctata (Benth.) R.Br. ex Briq. Currently named Micromeria imbricata var. imbricata, is a flowering, an erect, perennial herb that grows 10-100 cm in height and is traditionally used in h...

item.title: Impact of Continuous Nursing Intervention Delivered via Internet+ Platform on Inflammatory Responses in Ulcerative Colitis Patients.
item.description: Patient preference and adherence - To explore the application effect of continuity nursing intervention based on the "Internet +" platform in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) and analyze its impact on patients' inflammatory res...

item.title: Stromal state plasticity defines risk and therapeutic opportunity in recalcitrant inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) remain a relapsing, treatment-refractory disorder marked by progressive tissue injury and inflammation despite expanding immune-targeted therapies. We established ...

item.title: An Immune Microenvironment-Integrated Intestinal-on-a-Chip Model for Investigating Immunopathogenesis in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Molecules (Basel, Switzerland) - Owing to the multifactorial nature of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) pathogenesis, conventional two-dimensional (2D) models inadequately recapitulate the complex in vivo microenvironment. This st...

item.title: Understanding the Tripartite Relationship Between Dietary Practices, Psychological Well-Being, and Disease Experience in Greek Patients with IBD: A Mixed-Methods Exploration.
item.description: Nutrients - Background/Objectives: Diet and mental health constitute two significant modifiable factors affecting Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD). The present exploratory study explores potential interrelatio...
